result:
EXPLAIN shows query plan — how DB will execute SQL without running it.

Tells you:
- access method per table (seq scan, index scan, index-only scan)
- join order and join algorithm (nested loop, hash, merge)
- estimated row counts and cost per step
- filter and sort operations, whether sort spills to disk
- index usage — or lack of it

EXPLAIN ANALYZE actually runs query, adds real timings and actual row counts next to estimates. Gap between estimated and actual rows = stale stats or bad selectivity guess.

Use it to spot: seq scans on big tables, nested loops over huge row counts, sorts without supporting index, estimates off by 10x+.

Postgres and MySQL differ in output format but concept is same.
